reason: Despite significant institutional inflows into Bitcoin ETFs and its demonstrated resilience against geopolitical turmoil (outperforming gold and holding firm while altcoins slide), the price has struggled to gain substantial ground above $71,000. This divergence—high demand not translating to price appreciation—suggests strong selling pressure or supply absorption around current levels, indicative of a distribution phase. The immediate outlook is uncertain, with potential for increased volatility as buyers and sellers contend for dominance.
headlines: Bitcoin jumps above $71,000, building on its resilience to Middle East conflict
Ray Dalio says 'there is only one gold' as bitcoin outperforms gold during worst geopolitical crisis in years
Over a billion flows into bitcoin ETFs, yet the price isn’t rising — an analyst explains why
Bitcoin holds, ether, solana slide as Mideast woes drag Asian equities to multi-year lows
Ripple expands payments platform into end-to-end stablecoin infrastructure as processed volume tops $100 billion
